Details: One study investigated GAC isotherm for cyanide in drinking water, with an initial cyanide concentration of 1.0 mg/L [1367]. The sorption process proceeds slowly. After 33.5 hours of agitation, the system reached a pseudoequilibrium. The cyanide absorbed to GAC was 85 µmol/g of GAC. The study also suggest that other ions, including calcium(II), magnesium(II), iron(III), aluminum(III) and humic acid can inhibit the sorption of cyanide to GAC.
